findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "scan_website" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Run a free SimplyScan security & speed scan of a deployed web app URL. Returns a 0-100 score and findings (exposed secrets, missing Supabase RLS, frontend leaks…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "check_exposed_files" references sensitive artifacts (.env). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Probe a site for accidentally exposed high-risk files (.env, .git/config, backups, etc.). Reports HTTP status only, never file contents.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_security_headers, check_seo, check_email_security · Private data: scan_website, check_exposed_files · Public sink: check_email_security · Destructive: scan_website.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_security_headers, check_seo, check_email_security · Private data: scan_website, check_exposed_files · Public sink: check_email_security · Destructive: scan_website

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
